tries5 = 15 | goals5 = 0 | fieldgoals5 = 0 | points5 = 60 | retired = no | updated = 27 August 2025 }} }} '''Guy Richie Armitage''' (born 29 November 1991) is a [[Rugby converts|dual code]] English rugby footballer who plays [[rugby league]] for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]]. He previously played for the [[London Broncos]]. ==Background== Armitage was born in [[Westminster]], [[London]], [[England]].{{cite web |url=http://www.espn.co.uk/england/rugby/player/128430.html |title=Guy Armitage profile |publisher=ESPN Scrum |accessdate=21 July 2013}} He is of Trinidadian descent through his mother.{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/west-indies-rugby-league-team|title=How the West Indies could line-up if their rugby league team returned|date=10 January 2023|website=Love Rugby League}} ==Rugby union== Armitage played at [[Brixham Rugby Club|Brixham]] and earned a scholarship to [[Wellington College, Berkshire|Wellington College]] before joining the [[London Irish]] Academy.{{cite web |url=http://www.london-irish.com/PlayerDisplay.ink?skip=1&squadno=44607&season=13/14&seasonl=2013/2014&Playertype=p |title=Playing squad 2013–14: Guy Armitage |publisher=London Irish |accessdate=21 July 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140116183711/http://www.london-irish.com/PlayerDisplay.ink?skip=1&squadno=44607&season=13%2F14&seasonl=2013%2F2014&Playertype=p |archive-date=16 January 2014 |url-status=usurped }} He made his debut against [[Sale Sharks]] on 5 November 2010 becoming the third Armitage brother to play for the club after [[Delon Armitage|Delon]] and [[Steffon Armitage|Steffon]].{{cite web |url=http://www.london-irish.com/MatchReport.ink?SportStatMatchID=167514&type=m&storytype= |title=Sale Sharks 39–14 London Irish |publisher=London Irish |accessdate=21 July 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101217225733/http://london-irish.com/MatchReport.ink?SportStatMatchID=167514&type=m&storytype= |archive-date=17 December 2010 |url-status=usurped }} He then spent time on loan at [[RFU Championship|Championship]] sides [[London Welsh RFC|London Welsh]] and [[London Scottish F.C.|London Scottish]] during the 2010–11 and 2011–12 seasons, making a total of six appearances and scoring one try.{{cite web |url=http://www.itsrugby.co.uk/player_22167.html |title=Players profiles: Guy Armitage |publisher=itsrugby.co.uk |accessdate=21 July 2013}} He also made eight appearances for Welsh during 2011–12 but decided to join French side [[RC Toulonnais|Toulon]] at the end of the season.{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/17275729 |title=London Irish centre Guy Armitage confirms Toulon move |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=21 July 2013}} Less than six months after leaving, he returned to London Irish on loan for the rest of the season having not made any appearances in France.{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/19908522 |title=Guy Armitage relishing London Irish return |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=21 July 2013}} He played 21 times upon his return and in February 2013 re-signed with the club until 2015.{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/21352906 |title=Guy Armitage signs two-year London Irish deal |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=21 July 2013}} Before returning to London Welsh in 2015.{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/32448089 |title=London Welsh agree deals for Armitage, Drauniniu and Thomas |publisher=BBC Sport |accessdate=23 January 2016}} It was announced on 15 March 2016 that Armitage will join [[Aviva Premiership|Premiership]] side [[Wasps RFC|Wasps]] next season.{{cite news|title=Guy Armitage: London Welsh centre to join Wasps next season|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/35812039|publisher=British Broadcasting Corporation|accessdate=15 March 2016}} ==Rugby league== ===London Broncos=== Armitage signed with the [[London Broncos]] in August 2019 following a successful trial with the club.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/49194205 |title=Guy Armitage:London Broncos sign former London Irish & Wasps winger |publisher=BBC Sport |date=1 August 2019}} He made his Super League debut in round 26 of the [[2019 Super League season]] vs [[Catalans Dragons]].{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-ward-praises-forward-duo-after-impressive-catalans-victory/ |title=Danny Ward praises forward duo after impressive Catalans victory |publisher=Love Rugby League |date=18 August 2019}} In September 2020 it was announced that Armitage had signed for new Canadian club [[Ottawa Aces]] for their inaugural season in the RFL pyramid in 2021.{{cite web|url=https://www.timescolonist.com/ottawa-aces-sign-former-england-under-20-international-guy-armitage-1.24204276|title=Ottawa Aces sign former England U20 international Guy Armitage|publisher=www.timescolonist|accessdate=21 Mar 2021}} ===Toulouse Olympique=== On 9 Mar 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.{{cite web|website=www.rugby-league.com|date=9 Mar 2021|accessdate=21 Mar 2021|title=Guy Armitage joins Toulouse on loan|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/57722/guy-armitage-joins-toulouse-on-loan}} After a three week trial he was signed on for the 2021 season. He made a try scoring debut against Sheffield Eagles on 27 June{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/sheffield-vs-toulouse/55082|title=Championship (Sky Sports)|website=SkySports}} and scored two tries on his only other appearance of the season at Bradford.{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/bradford-vs-toulouse/55141|title = Championship (Sky Sports)}} On 21 October 2021 Toulouse announced that Armitage had signed a new one-year contract and would be part of the squad for 2022, their first season in Super League.{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/a-la-une/le-to-prolonge-guy-armitage-jusquen-2022/|title = Le TO prolonge Guy ARMITAGE jusqu'en 2022|date = 21 October 2021}} On 15 October 2023, Armitage played in Toulouse Olympique's upset loss in the [[Million Pound Game]] against the [[London Broncos]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=15 October 2023 }} ===Bradford Bulls=== On 31 October 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a one-year deal.{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=31 Oct 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Toulouse Olympique outside back Guy Armitage makes move to Championship rivals|url=https://www.totalrl.com/toulouse-olympique-outside-back-guy-armitage-makes-move-to-championship-rivals/}} ==International career== Armitage has represented England Rugby Union at every age group and made four appearances for the [[England national under-20 rugby union team|under-20s]] in 2011. == References == {{reflist}} == External links ==
